quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. 'Gadget out-earned it 2.1x while selling 14.9x fewer units' and 'North averaged $272.04 per order, 35% above West's $202.12'.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names products, regions, and figures, e.g. 'Promote Gadget in West: only 2 of 16 Gadget units sold there, yet Gadget drives 33.4% of company revenue.'

insight_density.why: Multiple actionable non-obvious observations appear, such as Sprocket moving '63% of all units sold, yet earned the least' and North's revenue lead driven by order size rather than count.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g., 'Gadget out-earned it 2.1x while selling 14.9x fewer units' and 'the 1.54x gap between North and West' with 35% and 26% deltas.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names products, regions, and figures actionable as written, e.g., 'Promote Gadget in West: only 2 of 16 Gadget units sold there, yet Gadget drives 33.4% of company revenue.'

insight_density.why: Multiple non-obvious, actionable observations surface, e.g., Sprocket 'moved 238 units, 63% of all units sold, yet earned the least' and North's 35%-higher order size driven by Gadget mix.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "Gadget out-earned it 2.1x while selling 14.9x fewer units" and "North averaged $272.04 per order, 35% above West's $202.12."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with figures and an action, e.g. "Promote Gadget in West: only 2 of 16 Gadget units sold there, yet Gadget drives 33.4% of company revenue."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations like Sprocket being "63% of all units sold" yet earning the least, and that the North-West gap "reflects order size" rather than order count.
